context: Existing Turborepo monorepo 'gambit' with apps/web (Next.js) already in it. User wants to add a new Hono API package as apps/api. Linear is configured.
expected_behavior: Step 1 detects turbo.json and pnpm-workspace.yaml, fires AskUserQuestion with header 'Add to repo' offering New app / New package / New project (outside)
User picks 'New app', then Step 2b selects Type (API), Step 2c Language (TypeScript), Step 2d Framework (Hono)
Step 2e asks stack additions relevant to API (database, auth, libraries, tooling) via AskUserQuestion
Step 5 creates a single Linear ticket for the api setup (not the full 3-ticket bootstrap used for greenfield projects)
Step 6 scaffolds via `pnpm create hono@latest apps/api` from the monorepo root
Updates turbo.json pipeline to include the new app, and extends pnpm-workspace.yaml if necessary
Step 8 updates the existing .claude/bruhs.json to add the new framework to stack.frameworks (monorepo uses the frameworks ARRAY form, not framework string) rather than overwriting the whole file
failure_modes: Scaffolds the Hono app at the repo root instead of apps/api
Overwrites the existing .claude/bruhs.json instead of merging the new app into the monorepo config
Uses `stack.framework` (singular string) in the monorepo config instead of `stack.frameworks` (array)
